label: FAQ subject
helper: Name the product, service, company, policy, or topic this FAQ covers.
why: It defines the central subject and keeps every question within scope.

label: Brand or organization
helper: Enter the name readers should associate with the answers.
why: It ensures consistent naming and prevents the model from inventing an organization.

label: FAQ page type
helper: Select the context so the question structure matches the reader’s needs.
why: It changes the native question architecture, answer emphasis, and escalation logic.

label: Primary goal
helper: Choose the single most important job this FAQ should perform.
why: It determines which questions deserve priority and how the page should guide readers.

label: Target audience
placeholder: Who will read this page, and what do they already know?
helper: Describe the main reader, their context, and likely level of familiarity.
why: It controls vocabulary, assumed knowledge, question selection, and answer depth.

label: Must-answer questions
placeholder: List the questions the final page must answer.
helper: Add exact questions or topics that cannot be omitted.
why: It captures the user’s actual information needs instead of relying only on model inference.

label: Confirmed facts and answers
placeholder: Provide approved features, prices, steps, limits, dates, policies, or answer notes.
helper: Include only information approved for publication; unsupported details will be omitted.
why: It supplies the factual basis required to answer questions without fabrication.

label: Output language
helper: The complete FAQ package will be written in this language.
why: It controls the language of all public copy, metadata, and implementation notes.

label: Answer depth
helper: Sets the typical detail level while allowing complex answers to be longer.
why: It materially changes readability, page length, and the amount of explanation.

label: Overall page length
helper: Auto selects a useful length from the scope, audience, and question count.
why: It lets users control scope without requiring a technical word-count decision.

label: Page introduction
placeholder: Choose whether to include an intro
helper: A short introduction is used only when it helps orient the reader.
why: It controls a visible page element that may help complex pages but burden simple support FAQs.

label: Additional context
placeholder: Add any context or requirements not covered above.
helper: Use this for one-off constraints; leave it blank when unnecessary.
why: It provides one controlled place for important exceptions without creating miscellaneous fields.

label: Scope boundaries
placeholder: What is included, and where should the page stop?
helper: Define boundaries when the subject could easily become too broad.
why: It prevents irrelevant questions and keeps the page focused.

label: Existing customer questions
placeholder: Paste questions from support tickets, sales calls, comments, or search data.
helper: Real customer wording should guide phrasing when available.
why: It improves relevance and preserves authentic user language.

label: Target question count
helper: Auto selects enough questions to cover the topic without padding.
why: It controls page breadth and prevents either thin or unnecessarily long output.

label: Question categories
helper: Suggest category names or leave blank for automatic grouping.
why: It allows the output to align with an existing website or help-center structure.

label: Reader journey stages
default:
Before choosing
Getting started
Using the product
helper: Choose where readers are in their journey; select only stages that matter.
why: It helps order questions according to when readers encounter uncertainty.

label: Objections to address
helper: Include only objections that genuinely apply to the offer or topic.
why: It helps resolve real decision barriers without manufacturing fear or resistance.

label: Topics to exclude
placeholder: List questions, subjects, or comparisons that must not appear.
helper: Use this to prevent overlap, sensitive disclosure, or off-topic content.
why: It establishes negative scope and reduces accidental disclosure or duplication.

label: Brand voice
placeholder: Calm, direct, friendly, and technically credible
helper: Describe the established voice, or leave blank for clear and natural wording.
why: It aligns the page with the brand without requiring multiple overlapping style controls.

label: Answer tone
helper: Select the dominant tone; factual accuracy always takes priority.
why: It changes how direct, warm, formal, reassuring, or technical the answers feel.

label: Preferred terminology
placeholder: Terms, capitalization, product names, or wording to use consistently.
helper: Add approved terminology that readers should see.
why: It prevents inconsistent product naming and terminology.

label: Wording to avoid
placeholder: List phrases, labels, claims, or tones that should not appear.
helper: Use this for brand-sensitive wording beyond factual restrictions.
why: It protects brand positioning where specific phrasing is unacceptable.

label: FAQ call to action
helper: Use one proportional next step after the FAQ, or omit it when unnecessary.
why: It controls the reader’s next step without forcing a promotional ending.

label: Preferred CTA wording
helper: Add exact button or link wording, or leave blank for a natural suggestion.
why: It allows exact interface wording when an approved CTA label already exists.
display: Show when `ctaGoal` is not Auto or No CTA.

label: CTA destination
helper: Add the destination page; leave blank when no link is available.
why: It prevents invented links and makes the final CTA implementation-ready.
display: Show when `ctaGoal` is Contact support, Start or sign up, Book a call, View pricing, Read documentation, or Visit a page.

label: Primary search phrase
helper: Use only when a specific search phrase matters; never force repetition.
why: It supports search-intent alignment when the user has an established target phrase.

label: Related search phrases
placeholder: Add closely related phrases, questions, or named entities.
helper: Use these for natural coverage, not keyword stuffing.
why: It helps cover adjacent search language without requiring many separate keyword fields.

label: Search intent
helper: Auto infers intent from the page goal, questions, and page type.
why: It influences question order, metadata, and how directly answers address the searcher’s task.

label: SEO title notes
helper: Add words that must appear in the suggested SEO title.
why: It captures title-specific constraints without asking the user to write the complete title.

label: Meta description notes
placeholder: Value point or wording to emphasize
helper: Add optional direction for the suggested search description.
why: It allows control over the snippet’s emphasis when that message materially matters.

label: Structured-data output
helper: Generate implementation guidance or JSON-LD only when appropriate.
why: It prevents the system from assuming every FAQ page needs generated markup.

label: Canonical page URL
helper: Used only when a final public page address exists.
why: It supports accurate implementation notes without inventing a page address.
display: Show when `structuredDataMode` is JSON-LD if appropriate or JSON-LD always.

label: Publishing format
helper: Select the format best suited to the site’s publishing workflow.
why: It changes heading, accordion, link, and code presentation for implementation.

label: Sensitive topic area
helper: Select a regulated or high-stakes area so answers use appropriate caution.
why: It activates subject-specific safeguards without burdening ordinary FAQ pages.

label: Relevant jurisdiction
helper: Add the country, state, or region when rules depend on location.
why: It prevents location-dependent requirements from being presented as universal.

label: Approved policies or terms
placeholder: Paste approved refund, privacy, delivery, eligibility, or support rules.
helper: Use exact current policy information when answers must reflect formal terms.
why: It grounds policy answers in approved language and reduces contradictions with formal pages.

label: Sources and reference links
placeholder: Paste source links, document titles, or reference notes.
helper: Sources guide accuracy; unavailable material must not be presented as checked.
why: It supplies traceable support for factual or high-stakes answers.

label: Details likely to change
placeholder: Prices, availability, deadlines, feature limits, laws, or schedules.
helper: Mark details that need cautious wording or pre-publication verification.
why: It prevents volatile information from being presented as permanently fixed.

label: Privacy restrictions
placeholder: Information that must not be disclosed or requested from readers.
helper: Exclude personal, confidential, internal, or security-sensitive details.
why: It reduces privacy, confidentiality, and security risks in public-facing answers.

label: Claims and promises to avoid
placeholder: Unsupported guarantees, comparisons, certifications, or outcomes.
helper: Add restrictions beyond the template’s permanent truthfulness rules.
why: It captures brand- or industry-specific claim boundaries.

label: Support escalation route
placeholder: When and how should readers contact support or another responsible team?
helper: Provide the approved next step for cases the FAQ cannot safely resolve.
why: It enables a safe handoff for account-specific, disputed, or unresolved situations.
display: Show when `faqPageType` is Customer support, Pricing or billing, Policy or terms, or Product or service.
